headline: New Highmark Stadium Opens With Ribbon-Cutting Ceremony
writeup: The biggest story in Bills land on June 25 is the official ribbon-cutting ceremony at the brand-new $2.1 billion Highmark Stadium, marking a historic moment for the franchise and the Buffalo community. Governor Kathy Hochul joined Bills brass and fans to celebrate the completion of the state-of-the-art venue, though her attempt to lead a Bills chant fell hilariously flat. On the field, attention turns to Josh Allen, who not only continues to be championed as the NFL's best player but also revealed dreams of representing the USA in flag football at the 2028 Los Angeles Olympics. Meanwhile, concerns linger around the roster, with the Bills receiving a harsh reality check on offensive skill position talent and questions about Skyler Bell's minicamp absence, while troubling news emerged off the field as Bills legend Jim Kelly revealed he suffered a stroke. The offseason also saw notable developments with Joey Bosa's career reportedly likely over and Joe Brady opening up about being 'broken' when Sean McDermott was fired.
